req.title: Lab order routing (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall order a lab test; orders shall route to the patient's preferred lab when available. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.
error_pathways: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable

doc.reasoning: The violation was that test[3] (cancelling an already-cancelled order) had kind=negative but heuristic=STC. STC (State Transition Coverage) is a structural/positive-coverage heuristic and must not be paired with kind=negative. Fix: change heuristic from STC to NEG for that test, which correctly classifies it as a negative path exercising an invalid state transition. All other tests are preserved unchanged.

tests.name: test_lab_order_routes_to_preferred_lab
preconditions: Patient record exists with preferred lab set to 'LabCorp'
LabCorp is available and accepting orders
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with valid payload including subject, code, requester, and performer referencing preferred lab
expected: Order ID is returned in Location header
trace: R-HLT-068 – positive routing path, preferred lab available

tests.name: test_lab_order_denied_for_non_clinician_role
preconditions: Authenticated user holds role: receptionist (not clinician)
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with otherwise-valid payload using receptionist credentials
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with severity: 'error'

tests.name: test_lab_order_rejected_when_mandatory_code_missing
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with payload missing the mandatory 'code' element
expected: OperationOutcome returned with issue pointing to missing 'code'
trace: R-HLT-068 – negative path: missing mandatory element

tests.name: test_cancelling_already_cancelled_order_rejected
preconditions: A ServiceRequest exists with status: 'revoked' (already cancelled)
actions: PATCH /ServiceRequest/{id} attempting to set status to 'revoked' again
expected: OperationOutcome returned describing invalid state transition
trace: R-HLT-068 – negative path: conflicting state transition (revoke an already-revoked order)

tests.name: test_lab_order_falls_back_to_default_when_preferred_unavailable
preconditions: Patient record exists with preferred lab set to 'LabCorp'
LabCorp is marked unavailable in the routing table
A default fallback lab 'DefaultLab' is configured
actions: System evaluates preferred lab availability at routing time
expected: An extension or note indicates preferred lab was unavailable
trace: R-HLT-068 – boundary/error pathway: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable

  "reasoning": "The requirement covers lab order routing via /ServiceRequest with positive routing to a preferred lab, negative paths (permission denial, missing fields, bad state transitions), and boundary conditions. Five tests are designed: (1) EP positive – valid clinician order routes to preferred lab; (2) NEG – non-clinician role is denied; (3) NEG – missing mandatory element rejected; (4) STC – conflicting state transition (cancel already-cancelled order) rejected; (5) BVA – preferred lab unavailable triggers fallback to default. Each test is self-contained, uses fixtures/helpers as placeholders, and stays within 40 lines of executable code.",

      "executable": "import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/ServiceRequest"

@pytest.fixture
def clinician_headers():
    return <auth_fixture>(role="clinician")

@pytest.fixture
def preferred_lab_payload():
    return <build_service_request_payload>(
        subject="Patient/p-001",
        code="58410-2",
        requester="Practitioner/prac-001",
        performer="Organization/LabCorp"
    )

def test_lab_order_routes_to_preferred_lab(clinician_headers, preferred_lab_payload):
    resp = requests.post(BASE_URL, json=preferred_lab_payload, headers=clinician_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "active"
    performers = [p["reference"] for p in body.get("performer", [])]
    assert any("LabCorp" in p for p in performers)
    assert "Location" in resp.headers
    <teardown_resource>(resp.headers["Location"], clinician_headers)

import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/ServiceRequest"

def receptionist_headers():

    return <build_service_request_payload>(
        subject="Patient/p-002",
        code="58410-2",
        requester="Practitioner/prac-001",
        performer="Organization/LabCorp"
    )

def test_lab_order_denied_for_non_clinician_role(receptionist_headers, valid_payload):
    resp = requests.post(BASE_URL, json=valid_payload, headers=receptionist_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i["severity"] == "error" for i in issues)
